original_abstract: Ferroelectric (FE) semiconductors, such as BaTiO₃, possess a remnant polarization that facilitates the separation of photogenerated charge carriers. In this work, we demonstrate for the first time that BaTiO₃ nanocrystals can achieve significantly enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution and photoelectrochemical water oxidation via this ferroelectric effect. Tetragonal-phase nanocrystals were synthesized hydrothermally from TiO₂ and barium hydroxide with a 63% yield. When deposited as films on tantalum substrates, these nanocrystals exhibited water oxidation photocurrents of 0.141 mA cm⁻² at 1.23 V vs. RHE under UV illumination (60 mW cm⁻²). Systematic electric polarization at 52.8 kV cm⁻¹ normal to the film plane resulted in a twofold increase or a 3.5-fold decrease in photocurrent, depending on the field polarity. This polarization also shifted the onset potential by –0.15 V or +0.09 V and modified the surface photovoltage response. Furthermore, field exposure enhanced the H₂ evolution rate of Pt/BaTiO₃ by approximately 1.5 times and improved the selectivity of silver photodeposition onto the (001) facets. Notably, all ferroelectric enhancements were reversible and could be neutralized by heating the samples above the Curie temperature. These phenomena are attributed to dipole-induced modifications of the potential drop across the space charge layer. Leveraging the ferroelectric effect to tune catalytic activity offers a promising strategy for advancing solar-to-fuel conversion technologies.
humanized_abstract: Ferroelectric (FE) semiconductors, such as BaTiO₃, have a remnant polarization that can aid in the separation of photo-generated charge carriers. Here we report for the first time that BaTiO₃ nanocrystals have been made to have enhanced photocatalytic H₂ production and photoelectrochemical water oxidation due to the ferroelectric effect. Tetragonal-phase nanocrystals were hydrothermally synthesized from TiO₂ and barium hydroxide in 63% yield. Films of the nanocrystals were grown on tantalum and under UV light (60 mW cm⁻²) at 1.23 V vs. RHE (vs. the reversible hydrogen electrode) a photocurrent of 0.141 mA cm⁻² was measured. Following electric polarization at 52.8 kV cm⁻¹ along the substrate normal, the photocurrent was found to increase by a factor of 2 by applying a negative field and decrease by a factor of 3.5 by applying a positive field. The onset potential of the photocurrent was shifted by −0.15 V for the negative field or by +0.09 V for the positive field and the surface photovoltage response was altered following polarization. Similarly, the photocatalytic production of H₂ by Pt/BaTiO₃ and the selectivity of silver photodeposition on the (001) facets were both enhanced by a factor of 1.5 following polarization. The ferroelectric effects on the photoactivity of BaTiO₃ were fully reversible and could be diminished by heating the samples above the Curie temperature. Thus, the effects on the space charge layer caused by the dipole field have been made use of in order to realize an enhancement of the solar to fuel conversion efficiency.
